title: Kid readability and input accessibility
summary: Make controls, focus, copy, and readable pixels friendlier without changing the core handheld identity.

guardrails: Keep the completed alpha arc intact: do not reopen High Pass density, season three, biome six, broad planner UI, crafting, combat, or inventory expansion during this campaign.
First stabilize feedback, review hygiene, deterministic proof, and player comprehension; content polish must be evidence-led or tightly scoped to existing surfaces.
Preserve the 256x160 handheld readability budget; prefer cuts, visual payoff, and existing seams over new pages or longer copy.
Respect lane ownership: lane 1 state/tooling, lane 2 science/copy, lane 3 spatial/visual, lane 4 route/support/replay. Shared-file edits need handoff notes.
Every main item must leave tests, browser proof, or a clear dated report; every critic item must either clear the lane or queue a narrow follow-up.
Avoid source-control conflicts by advancing each lane on its own dependency chain and using the scout report to name exact files before implementation.
completion_gate: A kid can discover controls, recover from overlays, and read core copy without external instruction.

lane-1.name: Systems / station / progression / tooling
lane-1.focus: Audit focus order, Esc behavior, fullscreen/menu exits, and save-safe input defaults.
lane-1.owns: runtime state, station surfaces, save safety, validation, packaging, and release tooling
lane-1.avoids: authored science copy, biome geometry density, and route prose unless a packet explicitly calls for a systems seam
lane-1.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-2.name: Content / journal / science / copy budgets
lane-2.focus: Review core instructional copy for age 7-10 readability and remove jargon where it is not teaching useful science.
lane-2.owns: science-safe content, notebook/atlas language, source-ledger coverage, content-quality tests, and kid-readable synthesis
lane-2.avoids: movement geometry, station state machines, and route controller behavior
lane-2.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-3.name: Spatial / traversal / visual-place feel
lane-3.focus: Review contrast, pixel silhouettes, and interaction markers on the most important route/clue frames.
lane-3.owns: world-space readability, traversal beats, environmental silhouettes, screenshot proof, and biome physical memory
lane-3.avoids: route state, copy-heavy notebook changes, station chrome, and save schema changes
lane-3.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-4.name: Route loop / support / replay / full-arc proof
lane-4.focus: Harden route notices and support prompts so failure/recovery states give one clear next action.
lane-4.owns: Route v2 behavior, support differentiation, notebook filing loops, route notices, replay windows, and deterministic route smoke tests
lane-4.avoids: new station pages, broad copy rewrites, raw biome geometry, and new route frameworks
lane-4.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

finding: Guided menu focus already defaults to world-map in biome play and field-station on the world map during station-return guidance.
Escape already closes normal menus and cancels reset confirmation before destructive reset actions.
toggleFullscreen currently persists settings.fullscreen=true even when canvas.requestFullscreen is unavailable, leaving a save-safe input-default gap.
The runtime-smoke fake DOM has no Fullscreen API, making it a focused harness for the unsupported fullscreen path.
main_scope: Update toggleFullscreen so unsupported fullscreen entry does not persist settings.fullscreen=true.
Keep fullscreen request failure or rejection from stranding the saved fullscreen setting as true.
Add a focused runtime-smoke regression proving unsupported fullscreen activation leaves settings.fullscreen false and Escape still recovers from the menu.
non_goals: No menu action order changes, guided menu default changes, reset-flow redesign, station/route/world-map behavior changes, save schema changes, authored copy changes, geometry changes, or UI layout changes.
lane_1_scout_refinement.known_unrelated_baseline_noise: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "covers title, play|arms sound" currently fails in the existing broad smoke because world-map focus expects forest and receives beach; do not make this packet depend on that broad smoke unless the map-focus issue is intentionally addressed.

result: Added an explicit fullscreen preference persistence helper.
Made unsupported fullscreen entry persist settings.fullscreen=false instead of leaving a stale true setting.
Made thrown or rejected fullscreen entry requests restore settings.fullscreen=false.
Added focused runtime-smoke coverage for unsupported fullscreen activation and Escape recovery from the title menu.
scope_notes: No menu action order, guided menu default, reset behavior, station behavior, route behavior, world-map behavior, save schema, authored copy, geometry, or UI layout changed.
Confirmed unsupported fullscreen entry now persists settings.fullscreen=false instead of keeping a stale true preference.
Confirmed thrown or rejected fullscreen entry requests restore settings.fullscreen=false.
Confirmed the focused runtime-smoke coverage proves unsupported fullscreen activation and Escape recovery from the unchanged title menu default.

Confirmed no menu action order, guided menu default, reset behavior, station behavior, route behavior, world-map behavior, save schema, authored copy, geometry, or UI layout changed for this lane-1 pass.

lane_2_scout_refinement.focus: Remove internal evidence-slot id wording from active field-request summaries while preserving route meaning and behavior.
implementation_scope: Update summary strings only where a player-facing field-request summary contains an exact evidenceSlots[].id token.
Add a content-quality guard that blocks route summaries from containing exact slot ids.
avoid: evidence slot id, slot order, entry id, route behavior, support behavior, station behavior, world-map behavior, save schema, geometry, menu/input behavior, or filed-note synthesis changes
rewriting label fields unless a focused test proves those labels are directly player-facing
broad content prose rewrite or science simplification

scope_notes: Replaced hyphenated internal evidence-slot id wording in the active field-request summaries with plain clue phrases.
Added content-quality coverage that rejects hyphenated evidence-slot ids in field-request summaries while allowing ordinary single-word clue terms such as shelter.
Refreshed focused exact-copy expectations without changing evidence slot ids, slot order, route behavior, support behavior, station/map/save/input behavior, geometry, or filed-note synthesis.
Confirmed the seven targeted active field-request summaries no longer leak hyphenated internal evidence-slot ids.
Confirmed the content-quality guard rejects hyphenated evidence-slot ids while allowing ordinary single-word clue terms such as shelter.
Confirmed no evidence slot ids, slot order, route/support behavior, station/map/save/input behavior, geometry, or filed-note synthesis changed.

finding: The lane-3 packet slice should focus on world-space readability, not copy or station/input systems owned by other lanes.
root-curtain is the highest-risk frame because it is route-critical, vertical, and becomes keyboard-nearest only in a small lower-climb band.
shore-pine is the useful front-half comparison frame because it is route-critical but approached from ordinary ground movement.
The existing tan/cream inspect marker reads, but it is soft on both dense forest and bright scrub/sand backgrounds.
main_scope: Add a one-pixel dark outline or shadow to drawInteractMarker(...) in src/engine/pixel-ui.ts while preserving its compact footprint.
Add or extend focused runtime smoke coverage proving root-curtain becomes the keyboard-nearest inspectable in the lower Root Hollow climb band before inspection.
Capture ignored browser proof for root-curtain and shore-pine marker frames after the contrast change.
non_goals: No route state, route copy, station UI, journal UI, save schema, content facts, field-request targeting, support behavior, world-map behavior, or biome geometry changes.
No inspect-range widening, target-priority rewrite, vertical-cue redesign, climb-hint redesign, NOTEBOOK J chip redesign, new text labels, arrows, animations, or second marker language.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "Open To Shelter outing|forest expedition slot|shows climb hints"

result: Added a dark in-footprint outline to the existing drawInteractMarker(...) shape while preserving its compact 9x9 handheld footprint.
Extended the Root Hollow runtime smoke path to prove root-curtain becomes the keyboard-nearest inspectable in the lower climb band before inspection.
Captured browser proof for root-curtain and shore-pine marker frames after the marker contrast change.

Kept route state, route copy, station UI, journal UI, save schema, content facts, field-request targeting, support behavior, world-map behavior, inspect range, target priority, and biome geometry unchanged.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "Open To Shelter outing|forest expedition slot|shows climb hints"

confirmed: drawInteractMarker(...) keeps its compact 9x9 footprint and only adds a dark in-footprint outline.
The Root Hollow runtime smoke path proves root-curtain can become the keyboard-nearest inspectable in the lower climb band before inspection.
Browser proof state snapshots confirm the selected root-curtain and shore-pine marker frames target the intended inspectables.

No route state, route copy, station UI, journal UI, save schema, content facts, field-request targeting, support behavior, world-map behavior, inspect range, target priority, vertical cue, climb hint, or biome geometry changed.
lane_3_critic_result.lane_3_status: clear; packet 147 complete for lane 3; promote ECO-20260420-scout-400
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "Open To Shelter outing|forest expedition slot|shows climb hints"
jq state checks for root-curtain and shore-pine marker browser proof artifacts

finding: Route-ready notices already give a clear station-return filing action; Shore Shelter also names M -> Field station plus Enter.
The remaining lane-4 kid-readability gap is the support-toggle notice copy in getOutingSupportNoticeText(...).
The hand-lens support toast currently says notebook-fit, exposing an internal helper phrase instead of a kid-facing action.
main_scope: Replace the hand-lens support toast with short, plain action language such as Highlights next notebook clue.
Optionally tighten note-tabs wording if it improves clarity, while keeping every support toast at five words or fewer.
Update the existing support notice copy test and add a guard against notebook-fit or hyphenated helper terms in support-toggle notices.
non_goals: No support id, support cycling, support unlock, route targeting, route definition, filed-note synthesis, station page, world-map focus, save schema, notice timer, geometry, or broad content copy changes.
Do not rewrite inspect-bubble Notebook fit labels in this pass; this packet slice is only the support-toggle toast.

result: Changed hand-lens support-toggle copy from Tags notebook-fit clues. to Highlights notebook clues.
Changed note-tabs support-toggle copy from Keeps notebook aim visible. to Keeps route aim visible.
Extended the focused support notice test to reject notebook-fit and hyphenated helper terms while preserving the five-word ceiling.
scope_notes: No support ids, support cycling, support unlocks, route targeting, route definitions, station pages, world-map focus, save schema, notice timers, geometry, filed-note synthesis, broad content copy, or inspect-bubble Notebook fit labels changed.
Confirmed hand-lens support-toggle copy no longer exposes notebook-fit.
Confirmed all support-toggle notices stay at five words or fewer and the focused test rejects notebook-fit plus hyphenated helper terms.

Confirmed no support id, support cycling, support unlock, route targeting, route definition, station page, world-map focus, save schema, notice timer, geometry, filed-note synthesis, broad content copy, or inspect-bubble Notebook fit label changed.

collision_rules: Lane 1 may touch large coordinator and station files; other lanes should not edit those files in the same packet unless a scout report queues the dependency.
Lane 2 may touch authored content and copy; lane 4 should not rewrite lane-2 prose while route behavior is in flight.
Lane 3 may touch physical placement and visual proof; it should not add route objectives or journal copy as part of geometry work.
Lane 4 may touch route/support/replay behavior; it should not create new station pages, broad content facts, or unrelated geometry.
If two lanes need the same mixed content module, the earlier scout must either sequence the edits or ask lane 1 for a data split before both mains proceed.
proof_expectations: At least one automated test, browser proof, or dated report for every main item.
Critic must record whether handheld readability, source accuracy, and lane ownership stayed intact.
Any follow-up must be narrow, named, and assigned to the owning lane rather than left as general polish.
